summary: Both changes are correct and minimal. Dashboard CATEGORY_OPTIONS values now use title-case strings matching the API's PRODUCT_CATEGORIES Zod enum exactly (Leather Goods, Jewelry, Watches, Fashion, Accessories, Other). Seed upsert update clause now includes passwordHash alongside role, so reseeding correctly updates the admin password. Build, typecheck, and tests all passed. No security regressions or code quality issues.

codeReview.summary: Two-file fix: (1) apps/dashboard/src/app/dashboard/products/new/page.tsx — CATEGORY_OPTIONS values changed from kebab-case (e.g. 'leather-goods') to title-case (e.g. 'Leather Goods') matching the API's PRODUCT_CATEGORIES array. (2) apps/api/prisma/seed.ts — upsert update clause changed from { role: 'ADMIN' } to { role: 'ADMIN', passwordHash } so reseeding updates the password. Both changes are correct, minimal, and well-targeted.

issues.description: Feature description lists 10 categories (Watches, Jewelry, Handbags, Leather Goods, Clothing, Footwear, Accessories, Fragrances, Eyewear, Other) but the API only validates 6 (Leather Goods, Jewelry, Watches, Fashion, Accessories, Other). The worker correctly matched the actual API validation rather than the description. This is the right call — the description was inaccurate, not the implementation. If the full 10 categories are intended, both the API's PRODUCT_CATEGORIES and the dashboard must be updated together.

Feature description specifies 10 category values (including Handbags, Clothing, Footwear, Fragrances, Eyewear) but the API PRODUCT_CATEGORIES only contains 6. Worker correctly matched the actual API. If 10 categories are intended, both API and dashboard need updating in a follow-up.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker feedback suggests making browser testing conditional in the frontend-worker skill for changes that don't affect visible UI (e.g. enum value fixes). The skill currently prescribes manual browser verification for all changes. For form value-only changes that pass build+typecheck, browser testing adds minimal value.

The PRODUCT_CATEGORIES list (Leather Goods, Jewelry, Watches, Fashion, Accessories, Other) is duplicated in create.ts and update.ts. This is a pre-existing pattern, not introduced by this fix, but could lead to drift if categories are expanded. A shared constant would be safer.
